note_snippet: PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Displaced left femoral neck fracture. POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Displaced left femoral neck fracture. PROCEDURE: Left total hip arthroplasty. Patient is a 74-year-old female who sustained a displaced femoral neck fracture after a fall.
POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Pathological fracture left femoral neck due to age-related osteoporosis. PROCEDURE: Left cemented total hip arthroplasty. Patient has known osteoporosis.
PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Right displaced intertrochanteric femur fracture. PROCEDURE: Right hip ORIF with intramedullary nail fixation. No history of pathological fracture.
PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Right displaced intertrochanteric femur fracture. PROCEDURE: Right hip ORIF with intramedullary nail fixation. No history of pathological fracture.

Patient presents with bilateral knee pain and crepitus. POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Severe bilateral primary osteoarthritis of knees. PROCEDURE: Left total knee arthroplasty. Right knee to be addressed in staged procedure.

Patient presents with bilateral knee pain and crepitus. POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Severe bilateral primary osteoarthritis of knees. PROCEDURE: Left total knee arthroplasty. Right knee to be addressed in staged procedure.
ASSESSMENT: Patient admitted with acute ST-elevation myocardial infarction of left anterior descending artery. Underwent emergent PCI with drug-eluting stent placement to LAD.
ASSESSMENT: Patient admitted with acute ST-elevation myocardial infarction of left anterior descending artery. Underwent emergent PCI with drug-eluting stent placement to LAD.
DIAGNOSIS: Systolic heart failure due to hypertensive heart disease, current exacerbation. Patient has longstanding hypertension with now decompensated systolic HF.
DIAGNOSIS: Systolic heart failure due to hypertensive heart disease, current exacerbation. Patient has longstanding hypertension with now decompensated systolic HF.
HISTORY: Patient has a history of coronary artery disease. MEDICATIONS: Aspirin, atorvastatin, metoprolol, and DVT prophylaxis with enoxaparin. Patient is admitted for elective CABG.
ASSESSMENT: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic chronic kidney disease, stage 3b. Patient also has essential hypertension managed with lisinopril.
ASSESSMENT: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic chronic kidney disease, stage 3b. Patient also has essential hypertension managed with lisinopril.
ASSESSMENT: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic chronic kidney disease, stage 3b. Patient also has essential hypertension managed with lisinopril.
Patient with known type 1 diabetes presents with diabetic peripheral neuropathy affecting bilateral lower extremities. Also has mild diabetic retinopathy, right eye.
Patient with known type 1 diabetes presents with diabetic peripheral neuropathy affecting bilateral lower extremities. Also has mild diabetic retinopathy, right eye.
POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Acute appendicitis without rupture. PROCEDURE PERFORMED: Laparoscopic appendectomy. No evidence of perforation intraoperatively. Patient doing well postoperatively.
POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Acute appendicitis without rupture. PROCEDURE PERFORMED: Laparoscopic appendectomy. No evidence of perforation intraoperatively. Patient doing well postoperatively.
POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Cholelithiasis with acute cholecystitis. PROCEDURE: Laparoscopic cholecystectomy. Gallbladder was inflamed, stones confirmed. Patient tolerated procedure well.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY: Breast cancer (treated, in remission 3 years). CURRENT DIAGNOSIS: Pathological fracture lumbar vertebra L2 secondary to metastatic disease. Known bone metastases from prior breast cancer.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY: Breast cancer (treated, in remission 3 years). CURRENT DIAGNOSIS: Pathological fracture lumbar vertebra L2 secondary to metastatic disease. Known bone metastases from prior breast cancer.

MEDICATIONS: Warfarin for atrial fibrillation, metformin for type 2 diabetes, lisinopril for hypertension, enoxaparin DVT prophylaxis perioperatively, proton pump inhibitor. ASSESSMENT: Admitted for elective right knee replacement.

MEDICATIONS: Warfarin for atrial fibrillation, metformin for type 2 diabetes, lisinopril for hypertension, enoxaparin DVT prophylaxis perioperatively, proton pump inhibitor. ASSESSMENT: Admitted for elective right knee replacement.

Patient has pathological fracture of the left femoral neck due to osteoporosis. Also has history of osteoporosis without fracture per old records. Current imaging confirms acute pathological fracture.
Patient presents with acute hip pain, left side. X-ray does not show fracture. MRI deferred. ASSESSMENT: Left hip pain, etiology unclear. Rule out labral tear vs early OA.
Patient received DVT prophylaxis with sequential compression devices and enoxaparin 40mg daily postoperatively following total hip arthroplasty. No clinical or radiological evidence of DVT.
Patient with confirmed bilateral DVT by ultrasound, treated with therapeutic anticoagulation. Heparin drip started, transitioned to warfarin. DVT confirmed in bilateral femoral veins.
Patient with confirmed bilateral DVT by ultrasound, treated with therapeutic anticoagulation. Heparin drip started, transitioned to warfarin. DVT confirmed in bilateral femoral veins.

ASSESSMENT: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic peripheral angiopathy without gangrene. Patient has rest pain in bilateral feet, ABI 0.6, confirmed peripheral arterial disease secondary to diabetes.
DIAGNOSIS: Type 2 diabetes with nonproliferative diabetic retinopathy, moderate severity, left eye, with macular edema.
Patient denies chest pain. No shortness of breath. No fever or chills. ASSESSMENT: No evidence of pneumonia on chest X-ray. Patient admitted for elective hernia repair.
HISTORY: No history of diabetes. Not a known hypertensive. ASSESSMENT: Admitted for acute pyelonephritis. Urine culture positive for E. coli.
HISTORY: No history of diabetes. Not a known hypertensive. ASSESSMENT: Admitted for acute pyelonephritis. Urine culture positive for E. coli.

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Left shoulder rotator cuff tear, full thickness. POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Full-thickness tear of left supraspinatus tendon confirmed arthroscopically. PROCEDURE: Left shoulder arthroscopic rotator cuff repair.

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Left shoulder rotator cuff tear, full thickness. POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Full-thickness tear of left supraspinatus tendon confirmed arthroscopically. PROCEDURE: Left shoulder arthroscopic rotator cuff repair.
Patient sustained injury to right wrist. X-ray shows displaced fracture of distal radius, right side. PROCEDURE: ORIF right distal radius.
Patient sustained injury to right wrist. X-ray shows displaced fracture of distal radius, right side. PROCEDURE: ORIF right distal radius.
Patient involved in MVC. INJURIES: Displaced left femoral shaft fracture, non-displaced right tibial shaft fracture. Both treated surgically. PROCEDURES: Left femur IM nail, Right tibia IM nail.

POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Lumbar spinal stenosis L4-L5 with neurogenic claudication. PROCEDURE: L4-L5 lumbar decompression laminectomy. Patient had significant leg pain and weakness preoperatively.

ASSESSMENT: Sepsis due to pneumonia caused by Streptococcus pneumoniae. Patient meets sepsis criteria â€” SIRS + suspected infection source. Blood cultures positive.

IMPRESSION: Possible pulmonary embolism. CT pulmonary angiography ordered but results pending. Patient started on anticoagulation empirically pending confirmation. Rule out PE cannot be excluded.
POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: L4-L5 disc herniation with radiculopathy, left. PROCEDURE: L4-L5 left microdiscectomy. Patient had 3 months of left leg sciatica with positive straight leg raise.

DIAGNOSIS: Nondisplaced fracture of right patella. Treatment plan: Knee immobilizer and non-weight bearing.
Patient with bilateral hip osteoarthritis. Admitted for right total hip arthroplasty.
ASSESSMENT: Acute NSTEMI. Patient with chest pain, troponin elevation to 2.4. Coronary angiography shows multi-vessel disease.

ASSESSMENT: Permanent atrial fibrillation. Patient on long-term warfarin for stroke prevention.

ASSESSMENT: Type 1 diabetes with proliferative retinopathy, bilateral, with macular edema.
